title: Pressure
short: Force spread over an area, P = F / A, measured in pascals (Pa).
full:
Pressure is the force exerted perpendicular to a surface divided by the area over which it acts, P = F/A. The same force concentrated on a small area (a sharp knife) produces a large pressure; spread over a big area (snowshoes) it produces a small one.

In a gas, pressure comes from countless molecules colliding with the container walls, and it is linked to volume, amount and temperature by the ideal gas law, P·V = n·R·T.

The SI unit is the pascal (Pa); 1 Pa = 1 N/m². Atmospheric pressure is about 101 325 Pa.
